toolCalls.arguments: {"command": "cd /home/alex/.config && git add -A && git commit -m \"Local configuration updates\"", "explanation": "Commit all your local changes first", "isBackground": false}

parts.text: actually lets make this a branch called mac

message.text: actually lets make this a branch called mac

response.value:
The branch has been renamed to `mac`. Now you can:

1. **Add and commit your changes:**
   ```bash
   git add -A
   git commit -m "Mac configuration updates"
   ```

2. **Push to the new mac branch:**
   ```bash
   git push -u origin mac
   ```

This way your updates will be in a separate `mac` branch, keeping the `main` branch unchanged on the remote repository.

toolCalls.name: run_in_terminal
toolCalls.arguments: {"command": "cd /home/alex/.config && git branch -m mac", "explanation": "Rename the branch to mac", "isBackground": false}

toolCallRounds.response:
The branch has been renamed to `mac`. Now you can:

1. **Add and commit your changes:**
   ```bash
   git add -A
   git commit -m "Mac configuration updates"
   ```

2. **Push to the new mac branch:**
   ```bash
   git push -u origin mac
   ```

This way your updates will be in a separate `mac` branch, keeping the `main` branch unchanged on the remote repository.
